title: Why the first five minutes in your store decide the sale — Daniel Fox
meta_description: The pleasure a customer feels five minutes into a visit predicts their unplanned spending. From there, music shapes time perception, the sensory mix, volume, and brand fit across the whole experience.
schema: <script type="application/ld+json">{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"Article","headline":"Why the first five minutes in your store decide the sale","description":"How music shapes the full customer journey, from entry pace and dwell to the buying decision and perceived wait at checkout.","author":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox","alternateName":"Daniel Fox","jobTitle":"Retail Music Behaviorist","url":"https://danielchristopherfox.com"},"publisher":{"@type":"Person","name":"Daniel Christopher Fox"},"mainEntityOfPage":"https://danielchristopherfox.com/notes/first-five-minutes-in-store.html","about":["Retail music","Customer experience","Dwell time","Conversion"]}</script>
